summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a stellar ’76 show, highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s and an incendiary second set.
review: The recording is praised as one of the best‑sounding ’76 tapes, with every instrument sitting cleanly in the mix and only minor first‑set quirks. Musically the concert shines – the second half of the second set (Help > Slip > Franklin’s, Dancing in the Street, Promised Land) is described as “on fire,” while the first set holds its own with strong renditions of Rain & Snow and Big River.

ai_rating.rationale: All seven reviews are overwhelmingly positive (average 4.7★), highlighting both superb soundboard quality and standout performances, especially the second‑set jams.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, intimate mix with every instrument distinct; archivists removed clicks/pops and balanced levels. A few reviewers note minor first‑set simulcast issues, but overall the sound is crisp and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is described as “on fire” with seamless transitions (H>S>F, Wharf Rat > Dancing > Sugar Magnolia). First set is solid and enjoyable, rounding out a well‑balanced concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and soloing receive high praise, especially on the Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence; even called for “Samson” during the second set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals highlighted as sweet and in‑tune, particularly duets like Looks Like Rain and Cassidy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s interplay with Garcia shines on Promised Land where they double up on chorus solos.
